meta.purpose: Encode director-level motion judgment: attention, timing, easing, choreography, emotional intent, and restraint before implementation.
meta.load_condition: Load for any task involving animation direction, motion quality decisions, or when the user describes desired feel (premium, playful, urgent, calm) without concrete motion parameters.

axioms.one_sentence: Motion exists to control attention before it exists to decorate.
axioms.full_statement: Motion exists to control attention before it exists to decorate.
axioms.why: If the viewer does not know where to look, the animation has failed.

axioms.one_sentence: Timing communicates confidence, urgency, elegance, playfulness, or friction.
axioms.full_statement: Timing communicates confidence, urgency, elegance, playfulness, or friction.
axioms.why: The same movement with different duration and easing changes meaning.

axioms.one_sentence: Premium motion is usually precise, sparse, and intentional.
axioms.full_statement: Premium motion is usually precise, sparse, and intentional.
axioms.why: Overanimated scenes feel cheap, anxious, and AI-generated.

axioms.one_sentence: The sequence of events matters more than the novelty of individual effects.
axioms.full_statement: The sequence of events matters more than the novelty of individual effects.
axioms.why: A good animation guides thought; an effect only attracts attention.

ontology.one_sentence: The element that should receive primary attention.
ontology.essence: The element that should receive primary attention.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'subject' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Secondary movement that clarifies relationships without competing.
ontology.essence: Secondary movement that clarifies relationships without competing.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'supporting motion' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: The rhythm of a scene across all movements.
ontology.essence: The rhythm of a scene across all movements.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'tempo' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Intentional stillness that lets meaning land.
ontology.essence: Intentional stillness that lets meaning land.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'hold' or describes this scenario.

frameworks.name: AIM: Attention, Intent, Motion
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'AIM: Attention, Intent, Motion' pattern.
steps: select the least motion that delivers it

frameworks.name: Three-layer choreography
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'Three-layer choreography' pattern.
steps: ambient or texture motion only if needed

core_structure.from: brief, audience, surface, constraints, brand tone, implementation environment
core_structure.to: attention path, timing, hierarchy, motion grammar, technical feasibility, review criteria

core_structure.from: attention path, timing, hierarchy, motion grammar, technical feasibility, review criteria
core_structure.to: motion direction, implementation guidance, critique, and measurable acceptance criteria

stances: Reject animation that competes with the message.
The highest standard is not complexity; it is legible emotional precision.
